findings.summary: Decoupling observation falsely reports U1 (DM02) has no +12V bypass caps; P5V_AUX and 3V3AUX classified as 'signal' nets instead of power; Component types, power rails (+12V, +3V3, GND), and BOM co...

correct.description: V2.0Beta: 10 components (4 connectors, 4 caps, 1 test point, 1 IC), 3 power rails, 201 nets (27 named, 174 unnamed). The large unnamed net count is expected — OCP and PCIe connectors have many pins without net labels in this design. BOM grouping is correct. Version evolution (V0.1Beta=12 comps, V1.0Beta=12, V1.1Beta=12, V2.0Beta=10) is tracked correctly across all four files.

incorrect.description: All four capacitors C1-C4 are wired to the +12V rail in the schematic. The analyzer reports rails_without_caps: ['+12V'] for U1 DM02 in every version (V0.1Beta through V2.0Beta). The root cause is that pin net_name is not resolved for KiCad 5 legacy .sch components — component pins show no net assignment (fields are absent), preventing the decoupling detector from linking caps to their power rails. The caps ARE there: C1 (6.3x5.4 electrolytic) and C2 (tantalum) are bulk caps; C3, C4 (1206) are ceramic bypass caps, all on the +12V bus. This is a false positive repeated across all four schematic versions.

P5V_AUX (5V auxiliary from OCP mezzanine connector) and 3V3AUX are global labels (GLabel type) in the KiCad 5 schematic. The analyzer classifies them as 'signal' because they don't use the power:* library prefix. Semantically both are power supply nets — P5V_AUX is present as a copper pour zone on B.Cu and F.Cu in the PCB, confirming it carries significant current. This misclassification means these rails are excluded from power_net_routing analysis. Technically defensible given KiCad's representation, but misleading for a board where P5V_AUX is the main supply net.

findings.summary: PCB statistics, routing, layer stack, and DFM analysis are accurate; J1 PCIe edge connector edge_clearance=-5.0mm flagged as warning — expected behavior for card-edge; P5V_AUX not analyzed in power...

correct.description: V2.0Beta: 15 footprints, 2-layer board (F.Cu/B.Cu), 112.05x78.25mm, 361 track segments, 195 vias, 10 zones, fully routed. DFM correctly identifies 0.127mm track spacing as borderline advanced tier. Board size exceeding 100x100mm correctly flagged for JLCPCB pricing. Courtyard overlap between J3 and C1 is a real design issue worth noting.

incorrect.description: J1 uses the Connector_PCBEdge:BUS_PCIexpress_x4 footprint, which intentionally extends beyond the board edge (negative clearance is by design for PCIe card edge connectors). The analyzer generates an edge_clearance_warnings entry for J1 at -5.0mm. This is a false positive — the board outline is correct and the connector is supposed to extend past the edge. The analyzer lacks awareness that card-edge connector footprints intentionally protrude.

missed.description: P5V_AUX has a copper pour zone spanning both B.Cu and F.Cu with 65.3mm² area and 16 stitching vias (thermal_analysis shows this). It is the dominant power net on the board by zone area yet is absent from power_net_routing and current_capacity analysis. This is a consequence of P5V_AUX being a global label rather than a power:* symbol — the PCB analyzer fails to recognize it as a power net, so no track width or current capacity analysis is done for this rail.

findings.summary: No gerber outputs exist — repo contains no gerber files

missed.description: The OCP2PCIe repo does not include any gerber/drill files (.gbr, .ger, .drl). The five gerber output paths referenced in the review request (OCP2PCIe_OCP2PCIe-gerber*.json) do not exist. No gerber analysis was possible for this repo.
